creator: mandree@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Mark Millard from comment #9)
So the code works as expected, given type widths, the results match what the prototype and K&R stuff suggests, it is value preserving in as much as the unsigned short int resembles what modern UNIX can store in uint16_t, in my code (that does not used "unsigned") as well as in yours.

Note that your format string does not match the parameters, you're using %hd which expects "signed short int", but you're passing an unsigned short int. Again, the value has been preserved.

I understand that the compiler has the right to complain about incompatible types in the prototyped forward declaration mismatching the K&R-style function definition, but also that the prototype gets things working because that's the state of affairs at the call sites, and later when the implementation follows the compiler warns but does the right thing. (in line with your comment #5 as I read it, and comment #8, ditto).

IOW the warning is valid but irrelevant because the forward-declaring prototype heals the mismatch of the late K&R-style implementation.

Anything important that I've missed?

So, can I close this PR with "Works as Intended" for its resolution, or is there something important that would make the code dangerous to use and that needs to change?  If the latter, I would like to see a sketch of what has to be done, preferably in the form of patches.
